subject: Q3 Budget Review - Engineering Department
content:
Team,

Effective Monday, we're updating the Travel Reimbursement policy. Key
changes:

1. Engineering approval is now required for any Legacy CRM changes in
production (previously only staging required approval)
2. All PRs affecting Vanguard must include unit tests
3. LATAM deployments will follow a new canary strategy
4. Incident response SLA updated: P1 = 12min, P2 = 69min

Full policy doc: https://wiki.internal/policies/487f1e03e5

Please review by EOD Monday and flag any concerns to Eve.

Thanks,
Olivia

--- This communication is intended for the addressee only. It may contain privileged or confidential information. If you have received this email in error, please notify the sender and delete this message. ---

subject: Re: Q3 Budget Review - Engineering Department
content:
Team,

I've been analyzing the p99 latency data from last sprint and noticed
some interesting trends:

- p99 latency increased by 22% compared to the previous sprint
- The LATAM region is underperforming by a factor of 2.9x
- We're seeing a correlation between p99 latency and conversion rate
- The Titan rollout appears to have had no measurable impact

Dashboard link: https://grafana.internal/ee6f4eaa3a

I think this warrants a deeper investigation. Eve, could you pull the
raw data from Payment Gateway so we can cross-reference with Legacy CRM?

Best,
Wendy
